text: A charity fundraiser has a target of £8. So far, they have raised £2.40. What percentage of the target has been raised, and how much more money (as a fraction of the target) do they still need?
generatorSolution:
Step 1: Convert £2.40 and £8 to pence: 240p out of 800p.
Step 2: Find the percentage raised: (240 ÷ 800) × 100 = 0.3 × 100 = 30%.
Step 3: Find how much is still needed: £8 − £2.40 = £5.60.
Step 4: Express £5.60 out of £8 as a fraction: 5.60/8 = 560/800 = 7/10.
Step 5: Check: 30% + 70% = 100% ✓
The answer is 30% raised, with 7/10 of the target still needed.
